vocab.word: be at one's beck and call
vocab.meaning: luôn sẵn sàng phục vụ/theo ý muốn của ai đó
vocab.example: I love being a passenger because I can simply enjoy the ride and have the driver be at my beck and call regarding the destination.

vocab.meaning: tận hưởng sự tự do/tự chủ
vocab.example: When I'm behind the wheel, I truly revel in the autonomy of choosing my own path without needing anyone's permission.

vocab.meaning: để tâm trí treo ngược cành cây/thả hồn theo mây gió
vocab.example: As a passenger, I can just gaze out the window and let my mind wander, which is incredibly therapeutic for me.
To be honest, the level of engagement you feel when driving a manual car is a cut above being a mere passenger.
After a grueling day at the office, I prefer to let someone else drive just to take the load off my mind.
I prefer being the driver as I like to be in full command of the vehicle's speed and safety at all times.
The sense of satisfaction I get from a perfectly executed gear shift is a notch above just sitting in the back seat.

vocab.meaning: mê hoặc/cuốn hút ai đó hoàn toàn
vocab.example: The open road has always held me in thrall, so I naturally gravitate toward being the one who drives.

vocab.meaning: gợi lên sự đồng điệu/thích thú
vocab.example: The idea of a hands-off commute really strikes a chord with me, especially during the hectic rush hour.
It is beyond all dispute that being a passenger is far less taxing than navigating through dense city traffic.
